response.justification: Bogdan presents a genuinely strong IC profile with rare breadth — distributed systems at scale (Dunnhumby, millions of req/s), current hands-on AI-agent and MCP infrastructure work at re:cinq, and consistent Staff-level titling across his career. However, Python is entirely absent from 15+ years of documented work and skills, which is a stated must-have for this role. His distributed systems depth is also several years dated, with recent years spent more on TypeScript product work. The AI-native exposure is real but only ~1 month deep. He clears the bar on craftsmanship, agile, and ownership mindset, making him worth a screening call to probe Python exposure and operational depth before dismissing.
strengths: Direct experience with high-throughput distributed systems (millions of HTTP req/s, async I/O, low-latency pipelines at Dunnhumby)
Current, hands-on AI-agent infrastructure work including MCP code generators and agentic workflow tooling at re:cinq
Long track record of Staff-level IC scope, craftsmanship practices (TDD, trunk-based dev), and public knowledge sharing across a wide technology breadth
concerns: Python is completely absent from the resume, skills list, and all project descriptions — this is a stated must-have skill for the role

High-throughput distributed systems experience is concentrated in 2018–2020; recent years have shifted toward TypeScript product and tooling work with less evidence of large-scale architecture ownership
AI/agentic environment experience at re:cinq started April 2025 (~1 month at time of resume), making it difficult to assess depth or strategic contribution

key_factors.factor: Active AI-agent and MCP infrastructure work
